decisions: Fixed stale guard file: memory/.last_consolidation contained 2026-04-22 despite consolidation reports existing through 04-25. Updated to 2026-04-25.
Retained all 4 daily logs (04-21 through 04-24): oldest is 4 days old, within 7-day window. All entries are heartbeat-only with no promotable content.
Skipped all tier promotions: every daily log entry documents heartbeat consolidation activity, not user interactions or learnings.
observations: Brain has been idle for 5 full days since bootstrap — zero user sessions, all memory tiers empty except episodic (2 auto-reflections)
Guard file was stale (2026-04-22) despite 7+ consolidation runs since then — indicates a systemic bug in prior consolidation runs that failed to update the guard file
Daily log entries are becoming formulaic ('still idle, nothing to promote') — this is expected for an unused brain but would be a concern if it continued after real sessions begin
recommendations: Future consolidations should verify guard file update as a post-condition — add explicit check that .last_consolidation matches today's date after write
If brain remains idle after 7 days, consider reducing consolidation frequency or adding a 'dormant mode' flag to skip redundant no-op consolidations
processImprovements: [self-critique] Guard file was 3 days stale — previous consolidation runs produced reports but failed to update memory/.last_consolidation. This caused the guard to trigger consolidation even though one already ran today. Need to ensure guard file update is never skipped.

[self-critique] Every consolidation for 5 days has been a no-op (no content to promote, no logs to delete). The consolidation process lacks a 'nothing meaningful changed' fast-path that would reduce overhead for dormant brains.

[proposal] Add a 'dormant' check: if no user-session commits exist since last consolidation, skip full tier review and just update the guard file + append a one-liner to TODAY.md. This would reduce processing time for idle brains while still maintaining the daily log trail.
